title: Validation, Release, Documentation, Recovery, and Portfolio Defense · Independent Product Review Board
summary: An unfamiliar small stakeholder product requiring prior-skill retrieval, ethical inclusive discovery, problem and outcome evidence, prototype choice, thin scope, reproducible repository, dependency review, architecture, invariants, responsive accessible interaction, threat and privacy analysis, layered tests, fault recovery, release identity, clean restore, user validation, documentation, demonstration, and independent claim defense. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across…
objectives: Map prior competencies to observable behaviors, dated artifacts, evidence strength, and unresolved gaps before selecting a product.
Create a revision-bound ledger linking each claim to artifact, verification, changed case, limitation, and owner.
Estimate who encounters the problem, how often, with what severity, and under which boundary conditions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run · Exam Competency Inventory
steps.instruction:
Order the Competency Inventory evidence cycle for this exam task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Plan the cumulative evidence run · Exam Competency Inventory.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Competency Inventory assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Plan the cumulative evidence run · Exam Competency Inventory, keep Competency Inventory connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A polished developer-machine demo has no immutable artifact identity, clean setup, user-outcome evidence, restored backup, explicit limit, or unseen defense. The exam variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map prior competencies to observable behaviors, dated artifacts, evidence strength, and unresolved gaps before selecting a product.

This does not satisfy Claim Defense Retro for released product evidence and public defense packet.
Stop release, localize the Claim Defense Retro cause in released product evidence and public defense packet, repair the smallest responsible layer, and rerun the full changed-case set.

Ignore the Claim Defense Retro boundary result because the familiar released product evidence and public defense packet demonstration passed. That claim fails the choose the regression defense evidence boundary for Claim Defense Retro.

steps.title: Submit the certification defense · Exam Claim Defense Retro
steps.instruction:
Write the exam evidence note for Claim Defense Retro: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A polished developer-machine demo has no immutable artifact identity, clean setup, user-outcome evidence, restored backup, explicit limit, or unseen defense. The exam variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Defend each mastery and impact claim with traceable evidence, answer counterexamples honestly, reflect on decisions, and define the next changed-context test.

Evidence target: Submit the certification defense · Exam Claim Defense Retro.
steps.why: Retrieving Claim Defense Retro through the concrete released product evidence and public defense packet case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Submit the certification defense · Exam Claim Defense Retro, keep Claim Defense Retro connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend each mastery and impact claim with traceable evidence, answer counterexamples honestly, reflect on decisions, and define the next changed-context test.

hints: For Submit the certification defense · Exam Claim Defense Retro, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Claim Defense Retro precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Submit the certification defense · Exam Claim Defense Retro, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A polished developer-machine demo has no immutable artifact identity, clean setup, user-outcome evidence, restored backup, explicit limit, or unseen defense. The exam variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Defend each mastery and impact claim with traceable evidence, answer counterexamples honestly, reflect on decisions, and define the next changed-context test.

For Submit the certification defense · Exam Claim Defense Retro, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Confidence and presentation polish are substitutes for evidence.
